title: Democrat wins Arizona attorney general race after recount
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — A recount of votes has confirmed Democrat Kris Mayes narrowly defeated Republican Abraham Hamadeh in the Arizona attorney general’s race, one of the closest elections in state history.

title: Biden outpacing Trump, Obama with diverse judicial nominees
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — For the Biden White House, a quartet of four female judges in Colorado encapsulates its mission when it comes to the federal judiciary. Charlotte Sweeney is the first openly LGBT woman to serve on the federal bench west of the Mississippi River and has a background in workers' rights.

title: New year expected to bring more changes to state voting laws
shortDescription: State lawmakers around the country introduced thousands of bills to change the way elections are run after former President Donald Trump falsely blamed his 2020 loss on voter fraud.

title: Jan. 6 panel drops Trump subpoena as it wraps up work
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Jan. 6 committee has dropped its subpoena against former President Donald Trump as it wraps up work and prepares to dissolve next week.

title: EXPLAINER: 2023 tax credits for EVs will boost their appeal
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Starting Jan. 1, many Americans will qualify for a tax credit of up to $7,500 for buying an electric vehicle. The credit, part of changes enacted in the Inflation Reduction Act, is designed to spur EV sales and reduce greenhouse emissions.

title: On the ballot in 2023: Southern governors, big-city mayors
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chicago Mayor Lori Lightfoot will be asking voters for a second term leading one of the nation’s biggest cities. Republicans will try to take full control of the Virginia Legislature.

title: Maryland Rep. Raskin says he’s been diagnosed with lymphoma
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Maryland Rep. Jamie Raskin said Wednesday that he has a type of lymphoma that's a “serious but curable form of cancer” and he is beginning several months of treatment. Raskin, who will be the top Democrat on the House Oversight and Reform Committee in the next Congress, said he expects to be able to work through his outpatient treatment at a Washington-area hospital.

title: Dubai approves extradition of financier accused of tax fraud
shortDescription: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A court in Dubai on Thursday approved the extradition to Denmark of a British financier accused of orchestrating a $1.7 billion tax scheme.Hedge fund trader Sanjay Shah is accused of masterminding a scheme that ran from 2012 to 2015 in which foreign businesses pretended to own shares in Danish companies and claimed tax refunds for which they were not eligible.

title: As Israel’s Netanyahu returns to office, troubles lie ahead
shortDescription: JERUSALEM (AP) — After five elections that have paralyzed Israeli politics for nearly four years, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has finally returned to power with the government he has long coveted: a parliamentary majority of religious and far-right lawmakers who share his hard-line views toward the Palestinians and hostility toward Israel’s legal system.

title: Wolf ends term with strong ratings after tumultuous times
shortDescription: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Gov. Tom Wolf will wrap up eight years in office next month, having steered Pennsylvania through unpredictable times when the Democrat made life-and-death decisions in the ravaging COVID-19 pandemic and managed the battleground state's presidential election amid unprecedented Republican efforts to overturn it.

title: Appeals court restores N. Carolina law addressing farm labor
shortDescription: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — A North Carolina law preventing legal settlements between farms and workers from requiring a farm operation to become a union employer doesn't violate the U.S. Constitution, a federal appeals court ruled Wednesday while overturning a lower court decision.

title: Massachusetts’ anti-Trump GOP governor ends time in office
shortDescription: BOSTON (AP) — Massachusetts Gov. Charlie Baker, an anti-Trump Republican who easily won reelection four years ago, learned his earliest political lessons listening to his Democratic mother and Republican father hashing out the issues of the day.

title: Appeals court halts federal takeover of Mississippi jail
shortDescription: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — Mississippi’s largest county won a legal victory Wednesday in its effort to stave off a rare federal takeover of its jail, where a judge has found “ongoing unconstitutional conditions” for prisoners.

title: 118th Congress to be sworn in, both parties will have slim majorities with split control
shortDescription: Without full Democratic control in both chambers, the Biden administration could face some uphill battles, like Republican legislation and possible investigations.

title: Arizona Republican Kari Lake appeals election challenge loss
shortDescription: Failed GOP Arizona gubernatorial candidate Lake is appealing her election challenge loss and a decision ordering her to pay Katie Hobbs' legal fees.

title: Trump tax returns will be released Friday, House panel says
shortDescription: A U.S. House committee is planning to release six years of former President Donald Trump’s tax returns Friday, a spokesperson confirmed to USA TODAY.

title: SCOTUS blocks Biden's ending Title 42
shortDescription: The policy, which has been in place since the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic, must continue while courts assess a lawsuit filed by 19 states.

title: Supreme Court blocks Biden from ending Title 42 migrant expulsions
shortDescription: The policy, which has been in place since the early days of the pandemic, must continue while courts assess a lawsuit filed by 19 states.

title: What's on voters' presidential wish list?
shortDescription: The leadership style, age, gender, experience and where their ideal candidate would call home, revealed in the latest exclusive poll.

title: Biden to hold airlines accountable after flight cancellations
shortDescription: About 4,000 domestic U.S. flights were canceled Monday, according to the tracking website FlightAware, which tracks flight status in real time.

title: Biden to spend New Year's holiday in U.S. Virgin Islands
shortDescription: Joe Biden will become the first sitting president to visit the U.S. Virgin Islands since Harry Truman delivered a speech there in 1948.

title: NY Rep.-elect Santos admits lying about career during election bid
shortDescription: Rep.-elect George Santos, R-N.Y., admitted that he lied about his credentials during his successful campaign for a seat in the U.S. House.

title: GOP stumbles with independents contributed to midterm election outcome
shortDescription: Republican House candidates nationwide won the support of 38% of independent voters in last month’s midterm elections, according to AP VoteCast.

title: El Paso city leaders call on Biden to visit the border amidst crisis
shortDescription: The mayor said emergency measure is "a band-aid on a broken system" and some council members say President Biden needs to see the crisis in El Paso.

title: Will Trump be charged in Georgia? A special grand jury will answer.
shortDescription: Will Donald Trump face charges in Georgia? A special grand jury is wrapping up its work investigating allegations of interference in the election.

title: This week: Congress avoids shutdown, Jan. 6 committee releases final report
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee ended their work this week with a final meeting and over 800-page report. Here's what else happened this week in politics.

title: Jan. 6 report: Trump floated plan for 10,000 troops to protect him - recap of findings
shortDescription: The House committee investigating the Jan. 6, 2021, assault on the U.S. Capitol released its highly-anticipated final report. Read it here.

title: Ukraine aid, pay raises, TikTok ban: Congress passes $1.7T spending bill
shortDescription: The package includes changes to retirement benefits, new protections for pregnant workers and Ukraine aid. A larger child tax credit was not included.

title: Congress revises Electoral Count Act that Trump used to pressure Pence on Jan. 6
shortDescription: Electoral Count Act reform, one of the top recommendations of the Jan. 6 committee, has passed Congress through the spending bill.

title: Senate sends $1.7 trillion spending bill to House: What made the cut
shortDescription: The bipartisan bill provides billions for Ukraine, pay raises for troops, and disaster aid. Extension of a pandemic-era child tax credit was left out.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: The IRS Hasn’t Released Nearly Half a Million Nonprofit Tax Records
shortDescription: If you’re trying to evaluate a charity this year, you might have a hard time. The IRS is behind on releasing Form 990s, limiting access to key financial information the public uses to evaluate the nation’s tax-exempt companies.

title: Republicans Step Up Attacks on F.B.I. as It Investigates Trump
shortDescription: Historically, the F.B.I.’s critics have come from the left. But the bureau’s array of inquiries into former President Donald J. Trump has turned the tables.By Adam Goldman and Alan Feuer

title: Jan. 6 Transcripts Shed New Light on How Trump Considered Blanket Pardons
shortDescription: The transcripts build on a growing body of evidence about the extent to which many in Mr. Trump’s orbit were seeking pardons after the 2021 attack on the Capitol.By Luke Broadwater, Catie Edmondson and Stephanie Lai

title: House committee staffers face no pay if speaker isn't decided by Jan. 13, officials warn
shortDescription: In guidance first obtained by POLITICO, House officials detailed how a delayed speaker vote would impact certain staff.

title: White House to Jim Jordan, James Comer: Sorry, but you have to restart your oversight requests
Senate GOP dealmakers depart just as Congress control splits
Santos scandal crashes into McCarthy speakership battle
Jamie Raskin announces cancer diagnosis
Santos struggles in Fox News interview about lying and integrity
